raw_response:
I agree with my initial assessment. Silicone impression materials indeed have strong adhesion characteristics post-curing, requiring either chemical dissolution or mechanical means such as scraping tools, both potentially harmful to the oral mucosa if improperly applied. This makes them particularly challenging to fully remove without risking injury to the patient’s gums or cheeks.

Answer remains B: Silicone impression material. Your agreement aligns well with our earlier conclusions about the challenges posed by silicones during removal procedures. They do present significant difficulties because of their firm adherence upon curing, necessitating careful handling to avoid complications. Therefore, sticking with option B reflects an accurate understanding based on clinical experience and material behavior. Answer confirmed: B.
